content: {"analyses": [{"source_url": "https://www.researchgate.net/publication/229578765_The_effect_of_damp-heat_and_UV_aging_tests_on_the_optical_properties_of_silicone_and_EVA_encapsulants", "source_title": "The effect of damp-heat and UV aging tests on the optical properties of silicone and EVA encapsulants", "source_type": "journal_article", "source_quality": 0.0, "overall_relevance": 0.0, "year": 0, "authors": [], "venue": "", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [], "evidence_summary": "INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT: ResearchGate returned security check/CAPTCHA page instead of article content. No extractable scientific text."}, {"source_url": "https://www.ise.fraunhofer.de/content/dam/ise/de/documents/publications/conference-paper/38th-eupvsec-2021/Oez_4AV15.pdf", "source_title": "The Impact of the Lamination Process on the Adhesion Properties at the Glass-Encapsulant Interface and Damp Heat Stability of PV Modules", "source_type": "journal_article", "source_quality": 0.82, "overall_relevance": 0.9, "year": 2021, "authors": ["Öz A.K.", "Herzog C.", "Wellens C.", "Mansour D.E.", "Heinrich M.", "Kraft A."], "venue": "38th EU PVSEC, Fraunhofer ISE", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "The relative maintained peel force at the glass-encapsulant interface after 3000 hours of damp heat exposure at 85 degrees Celsius and 85% relative humidity varied from 15.7% to 73.0% depending on the encapsulant material and lamination process parameters used.", "direct_quote": "The peel test results show that the relative maintained peel force (RMPF) varies in the range from 15.7-73.0% depending on the material and the lamination process. It is obtained that the EVA laminates showed less degradation in peel force after DH than POE samples and a higher lamination temperature was beneficial in peel force and gel content before and after ageing for cross-linking encapsulants such as EVA and POE. For the used TPO the peel force did not change for different lamination temperatures", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.95, "confidence": 0.92, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["RMPF", "peel force", "15.7-73.0%", "EVA", "POE", "TPO", "damp heat", "3000 hours"]}, {"statement": "TPO-encapsulated laminates with PET/Aluminium/PET/Olefin backsheet showed peel forces of approximately 160 N/cm before damp heat exposure, which dropped to 33-45 N/cm after 3000 hours of DH aging, representing a 72-79% reduction in adhesion strength at the glass-encapsulant interface.", "direct_quote": "the highest peel force degradation occurred at the laminates which used the PET/Aluminium/PET/Olefin backsheet with a TPO encapsulant. While these laminates had peel forces of around 160 N/cm before the damp heat exposure, they dropped to 33-45 N/cm after exposing them to DH for 3000 hours. However, the absolute values of peel force are still amongst the highest overall and at DH 3000 all samples failed because of a delamination within the backsheet", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.93, "confidence": 0.93, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["TPO", "160 N/cm", "33-45 N/cm", "3000 hours DH", "PET/Aluminium/PET/Olefin", "peel force degradation"]}, {"statement": "Laminates with PET/PET/Primer backsheet showed peel forces dropping from approximately 120 N/cm to 18-25 N/cm after 3000 hours of damp heat, while polypropylene backsheet laminates started lower at around 65 N/cm but degraded to only 30 N/cm, with degradation rates falling to 15% or less after the initial 1500 hours.", "direct_quote": "A strong drop in peel force is also observed for the laminates with the PET/PET/Primer backsheet. These laminates dropped from around 120 N/cm to 18-25 N/cm after DH 3000. The laminates with polypropylene backsheet on the other hand had much lower peel forces (~65 N/cm) before DH testing compared to the other two backsheets, but after the damp heat exposure they degraded less than the others and their peel forces dropped to ~30 N/cm", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.91, "confidence": 0.91, "perspective": "Industry", "entities": ["PET/PET/Primer", "120 N/cm", "18-25 N/cm", "polypropylene", "65 N/cm", "30 N/cm", "DH 3000"]}, {"statement": "Small-scale 200mm x 200mm laminates were produced using EVA, POE, and TPO encapsulants at peak lamination temperatures of 140, 160, and 180 degrees Celsius, with lamination times of 4-10 minutes, and damp heat tested at 85 degrees Celsius and 85% relative humidity according to BS EN ISO 8510 peel test standards.", "direct_quote": "Small scale laminates (200 mm x 200 mm) with polypropylene (PP) and polyethylene terephthalate (PET) based backsheets were laminated with ethylene-co-vinyl acetate (EVA), polyolefin elastomer (POE), and thermoplastic polyolefin (TPO) based encapsulants at peak laminations temperatures of 140 °C, 160 °C, and 180 °C. The lamination time varied between 4-10 minutes as shown in Table 1", "fact_category": "methodology", "relevance_score": 0.85, "confidence": 0.94, "perspective": "Methodological", "entities": ["200mm x 200mm", "EVA", "POE", "TPO", "140°C", "160°C", "180°C", "BS EN ISO 8510"]}], "evidence_summary": "Fraunhofer ISE study on lamination process effects on glass-encapsulant adhesion under DH aging up to 3000h. Key finding: RMPF ranges 15.7-73.0%. TPO peel force drops from 160 to 33-45 N/cm. EVA shows less degradation than POE. Higher lamination temperature benefits adhesion retention. Highly relevant to encapsulant adhesion degradation under damp heat."}]}
